legalDisclaimer.text: This platform facilitates case submission only. Final decisions are made by qualified Islamic authorities in accordance with Islamic law.
legalDisclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer

home.title: Start Your Divorce Case
home.subtitle: Begin your journey with a dignified, Shariah-compliant process. Select the type of divorce proceeding that applies to your situation.
home.trustText: All cases are reviewed by qualified Islamic scholars and processed according to established Islamic principles.
home.understandingText1: Dar-ul-Qaza provides a dignified, Shariah-compliant platform for resolving family matters according to Islamic principles. Our process ensures fairness, transparency, and adherence to Islamic law while protecting the rights of all parties involved.
home.understandingText2: The Islamic divorce process is designed to first attempt reconciliation (Sulah) between spouses. If reconciliation is not possible, the process proceeds through carefully defined steps, ensuring all legal and religious requirements are met before a final decision is made by a qualified Qazi (Islamic judge).
home.understandingText3: All cases are handled with the utmost respect, confidentiality, and in accordance with Islamic jurisprudence.
home.contactUsDesc: For questions about the process or case status
home.selectDivorceType: Please select a divorce type first

application.title: Application
application.description: Submit your divorce application with required personal and marriage details

resolution.title: Sulah (Resolution)
resolution.description: Islamic reconciliation attempt to resolve differences amicably

agreement.title: Agreement
agreement.description: Define terms of separation including mahr, iddat, custody, and maintenance

affidavits.title: Affidavits
affidavits.description: Submit sworn statements from applicant, witnesses, and supporting documents

review.title: Qazi Review
review.description: Case reviewed by qualified Islamic judge (Qazi) for Shariah compliance

certificate.title: Certificate
certificate.description: Receive official divorce certificate upon approval

talaq.title: Talaq
talaq.description: Divorce initiated by the husband according to Islamic law
talaq.details: The husband pronounces talaq, followed by a waiting period (iddat) and proper procedures.

khula.title: Khula
khula.description: Divorce initiated by the wife with mutual consent
khula.details: The wife seeks khula, often involving return of mahr or other agreed terms.

dashboard.title: My Cases Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Track every step of your Islamic divorce process.
dashboard.noActiveCaseDesc1: Start your first case to begin the process
dashboard.noActiveCaseDesc2: You have no active cases. Start a new case or view completed cases.
dashboard.unableToLoad: Unable to load cases. Please try again.
dashboard.completeCurrentCase: Complete the current case before starting a new one.
dashboard.greetingSubtitle: Your Dar-ul-Qaza dashboard provides a calm, step-by-step view of your case.

divorceForm.title: Divorce Application Form
divorceForm.subtitle: Please provide accurate information with calm and care. This is a formal Islamic judicial process, not an instant decision.
divorceForm.reviewText: Please carefully review all details below before submitting. This will be sent to qualified Islamic scholars for formal review.

resolutionForm.title: Islamic Resolution (Sulh) Attempt
resolutionForm.subtitle: Record the outcome of the reconciliation attempt between the parties.

agreementForm.title: Divorce Agreement Terms
agreementForm.subtitle: Define the terms of separation as per Islamic law and mutual agreement.
agreementForm.mehrPlaceholder: Details about mahr payment and settlement...
agreementForm.maintenancePlaceholder: Maintenance terms during iddat and beyond...

affidavitsForm.title: Affidavits Confirmation
affidavitsForm.subtitle: Confirm that all required sworn statements (affidavits) have been submitted and verified.
affidavitsForm.noteText: All affidavits must be properly notarized and submitted to the court before proceeding to Qazi review.

success.title: Success!
success.caseSubmitted: Your divorce application has been submitted for scholarly review. Qualified Islamic authorities will now review your application in detail.
success.caseSubmittedFull: Your case has been submitted for scholarly review. You will be notified of the next steps.

footer.aboutText: A dignified, Shariah-compliant platform for resolving family matters according to Islamic principles with fairness and transparency.
footer.serving: Serving the Muslim community with dignity, care, and respect.

signUp.title: Create Account
signUp.subtitle: Join Darul Qaza to start your case
